text.title: TDD Workflow Worked Example
text.summary: One worked behavior cycle plus CLAUDE.md and PostToolUse hook excerpts.

example.note: Step 1 (RED): write a failing test for "register issues a verification token".
Step 2 (run RED): `pytest -x tests/test_register.py` shows FAILED with NameError or AssertionError — confirm fail-for-the-right-reason.
Step 3 (GREEN): minimal implementation. NO branching for "edge cases not yet tested" (r3).
CLAUDE.md snippet (templates/claude-md-snippet.md) pins the discipline so the agent reads it on session start.
example.code:
## TDD Workflow
For every new function or method: 1. Write the failing test FIRST. Run it. 2. Confirm: test output shows FAILED with the expected error. 3. Write minimal implementation. Run tests again: must show PASSED. 4. Refactor if needed. Tests must still pass. 5. Commit both test and implementation together.
NEVER write implementation before a failing test exists.

example.note: Bad — implementation written first, then test added to confirm it (r1 violation). Detector: PostToolUse hook log shows no pytest run between Write(impl.py) and Write(test.py).
example.code: # 1) wrote register() to issue a token ← r1 violated def register(email): ... # 2) then wrote a test that already passes def test_register(): assert isinstance(register("a@b.c"), str)
